text: Pet Emporium (p. 15) 9. DMV Express (p. 15) 10. Skate World (p. 15) 11. Mootsy’s Arcade (p. 15) Level 2 12. Break Room (p. 16) 13. Security (p. 17) 14. Business Association (p. 18) 15. Emmy’s Swords & Stuff (p. 18) Level 3 16. The Manager’s Office (p. 20) 17. Food Court (p. 21) 18. Stan’s Pretzel Stand (p. 21) 19. Joe’s Burger Barn (p. 21) 20. Spiral Cinema (p. 21) Basement 22. Utility Room (p. 22) 23. Mall Rats Clubhouse (p. 22) 24. Bunker & Tunnels (p. 23) FACILITATING Become familiar with the mall’s layout, the NPCs(p. 6) and the DOOM clock(p. 2). Use the Character Creation (p. 32) steps to create PCs, get a rumor, introduce a custom NPC and create a shop for the mall. At the start of play it’s 7pm. The DOOM clock starts ticking and the crowd gathers at #1 The Contest Stage. Terry - Night Security (p. 8) Hudson - Head of Security (p. 7) FACTIONS & NPCS After the initial events (DOOM 1 - 3), factions quickly form. Each faction has broad goals and actions, while its individual members have varying responses to DOOM 3+. Most NPCs are listed under their chosen faction, while others are listed in Unaffiliated NPCs. To usher the Children of Ammon (p. 9) into our world, so they can prepare the final sacrifice. ▶ Actions: They kidnapped people to turn into Fruiting Bodies (p. 13) and build the portal below the mall. ▶ Base:Manager’s Office (p. 20) and the Tunnels (p. 23). ▶Mr. Neil (he/him) - Mall Manager STR: 14 DEX: 10 CTRL: 14 HP: 5 Armor: 1 Revolver (d6) �Middle aged, lean, constantly fidgeting, off putting. Wears ill fitting tan suits (muddy hems). �He has been REPLACED (p. 8). �Carries the Manager’s Office key and a vague letter apologizing to an unnamed recipient. �Crisis Response: Does everything in his power to convert more into Children of Ammon (p. 9). �Location:Manager’s Office (p. 19) or the Tunnels (p. 23). ▶ Billy (he/they) - Mr. Neil’s Assistant Switchblade (d6) �Dirty coveralls, clipboard filled with tasks, hair covering face. �Aided Mr. Neil in the kidnappings and sacrifices. �Crisis Response: Helps convert others. �Location: the Loading Docks (p. 14) or Access Hallways (p. 11). To protect their capital above all else. To assert control by maintaining old hierarchies. ▶ Actions: Will quickly resort to violence to assert order. They will fortify their office and destroy any nearby cameras before attempting to take the security office. ▶ Base:Business Association(p. 18). ▶ Maggie (she/her) - Coalition of Business Owners STR: 12 DEX: 14 CTRL: 13 HP: 4 Armor: 1 Revolver (d6) �Sharp suit, Gucci glasses, heels. �Was quietly organizing a fiscal takeover of the mall pre-disaster. �Crisis Response: Might makes right. Uses her pre-crisis position as leverage to lead during this crisis. Weakness will be squashed quickly and publicly. Will not hesitate to enact violence. �Locations: Fortifying the Business Association(p. 18). Gathering people who are of use. Taking over Security (p. 17). ▶ William (he/him) - Mall Walker �Older, Floral shirt, short shorts. �Retired young and has enjoyed every minute of it. �Crisis Response: Becomes extremely accusatory of others. �Location: Around the mall. ▶ Terry (he/him) - Night Security STR: 8 DEX: 8 CTRL: 8 HP: 3 Armor: 1 Taser (d6, nonlethal) �Middle aged, scowling. �Amassed contraband for himself pre-disaster. �Crisis Response: Vies for extreme measures. �Location: Breaking into stores. DOOM 5 leads charge in taking of Security (p. 17). AMMON’S CHOSEN COALITION OF BUSINESS OWNERS [06] Factions & NPCS // Maggie will lead her faction on a brutal campaign for control: Might = Right. Factions & NPCs // Mall Rats = save and survive; Griffith will use fear and superstition to amass power. [07] Movement & Wellness Clinic. Members wear matching pastel-purple tracksuits and white Nikes. Quickly shifts from wellness & movement focus to being an End of Days cult convinced of salvation through rebirth. ▶ Actions: Quickly collect resources (for the “greater good”). ▶ Base: Your Body Is a Temple (p. 14). ▶ Griffith (he/they) - Head Instructor �Warm smile and striking gray eyes. Sharp features. �His gray tracksuit has silver piping. �Exudes calm. Charismatic. �Crisis Response: It was a sign. Leverages devoted followers. Gives outsiders one chance to join the path before treating them as outcasts. �Locations: Coordinating his followers from within Your Body Is a Temple (p. 14). ▶ Samuel (he/him) - Evening Instructor �Stout, tightly cropped buzz cut. �Believes in Griffith’s vision, wants to make it a reality. �Crisis Response: Uses this as an excuse to mete out violence, manifesting Griffith’s desires with any means necessary. �Location: Leading teams to gather supplies. ▶ Hudson (he/him) - Head of Security STR: 11 DEX: 11 CTRL: 8 HP: 3 Armor: 1 Taser (d6, nonlethal) �Young, flustered. �Takes pride in his job, but isn’t very good at it. �Crisis Response: Freezes, unable to take action. Does not rise to the occasion. �Location: Crying in Security (p. 17), trying to find where in the manual it covers a situation like this. Without help, seeks out Griffith to lead him. YOUR BODY IS A TEMPLE To survive. To stick it to the business owners. Will attempt to avoid any other major faction and wait out the problem. Use the vent system to get around the mall (pre/post crisis). ▶ Actions: Gather supplies, build a group of “stable” survivors. ▶ Base:Clubhouse(p. 22). ▶ Beth (she/her) - Mall Rat STR: 10 DEX: 14 CTRL: 12 HP: 4 Armor: 1 Brass knuckles (d6) �Trans, flannel, t-shirt, baggy jeans, skateboard. �Constantly butts heads with management but is liked by employees. �Quick and rarely caught off guard.. �Crisis Response: Encourages others to avoid threats, stick together and get items necessary for survival. Helps others when push comes to shove. �Locations: Vents (p. 13). Seeking aid from the PCs to rescue her friend from the vent. Clubhouse(p. 22). ▶ Ruth (she/her) - Mall Patron �Golden Girls. �Used to work at the mall, knows all the goings on.
